description: ResyncCoordinator gap recovery + convergence (protocol.md § Reliable Sync, #resync-coord). A receiver drops an arbitrary suffix of deltas, detects the gap on the next delta (base_epoch > last_epoch), emits ResyncRequest, applies the covering Snapshot, and reaches the SAME graph state as a receiver that saw every delta — gap recovery is state-equivalent, not lossy (proven ReliableSync.resync_convergence). The decision function is the ResyncCoordinator.ingest table: base_epoch == last_epoch -> Apply; base_epoch < last_epoch -> Ignore (re-delivery); base_epoch > last_epoch -> RequestSnapshot { from: last_epoch }. ResyncRequest / Snapshot `wire` frames validate against schemas/reliable-sync.json and schemas/snapshot.json.

scenarios.name: drop_suffix_then_resync_converges
scenarios.description: Receiver A applies epochs 1,2 then MISSES the delta 2->3 (dropped). The next inbound delta 3->4 has base_epoch=3 > last_epoch=2 -> gap. A emits ResyncRequest{from:2}, the sender replies Snapshot{epoch:4}, A applies it. A's final graph equals receiver B's, which saw every delta 1->2->3->4.

inbound.note: delta 2->3 (CellSet node 2 = 20) never arrives at receiver A

scenarios.name: single_request_per_gap
scenarios.description: While resyncing (request outstanding, covering Snapshot not yet applied), further ahead-of-cursor deltas are Ignored and do NOT emit duplicate ResyncRequests. Bounds request storms under a burst.
